paragraph:
# Velmoria's recalcitrant province

*One hundred and ninety years on, a defiant region tests whether a republic can hold together without becoming a (tyrant)*

LET THIS chapter examine a crisis of unity. One hundred and ninety years after the marshal fell, Velmoria faced a (poignant) and dangerous test: a (recalcitrant) province on its northern frontier, long restless, now openly (defiant), threatening to (revolt) and secede. The (significant) question was whether the republic could hold itself together—whether it could answer a (recalcitrant) region without becoming the very (tyrant) its founders had overthrown.

The province had real grievances, and they were not (scant). Its people felt the central government (dominant) and remote, its policies dismissive, its tone (mordant). They were (verdant) farming communities whose interests, they believed, the capital treated as (insignificant). Their resentment had grown from a (dormant) murmur to a (vibrant), (rampant) movement. A (trenchant) regional leader gave it voice—an (itinerant) orator, (puissant) and (eloquent), who could (enchant) a crowd and (rant) against the capital with (incessant) energy.

The leader's case was (poignant) and not wholly without (merit). The province, he argued, had become a (tenant) in its own land, a (suppliant) before a (dominant) capital. Its people were tired of being (compliant), tired of the (exorbitant) demands and (scant) returns. He was no mere (sycophant) or (flippant) demagogue; his complaints were (significant), his (penchant) for the (trenchant) phrase matched by a real grasp of grievance. But his proposed remedy—secession, or failing that, (revolt)—was (aberrant) and (malignant), a cure worse than the disease.

Recall how the marshal had handled regional dissent. He was a (tyrant) who brooked no (recalcitrant) voice. He would (supplant) any (defiant) leader, (catapult) loyalists into power, and crush any (revolt) with a (rampant), (malignant) cruelty. He treated every province as a (tenant) at his pleasure, every citizen a (suppliant) before his throne. His answer to grievance was the prison (vault) and the (writ) of arrest; his covenant with the regions was no (covenant) at all, but a (mordant) (fiat). To be (recalcitrant) under the marshal was to (deposit) oneself in an early grave.

The republic, (chasten)ed by that memory, refused both the (tyrant)'s crackdown and the (pusillanimous) surrender. It would neither (catapult) an army northward to crush the (revolt) nor (recant) the principle that the republic was one. The challenge was to remain (vigilant) without becoming (malignant), to be (puissant) without being a (tyrant). The capital's leaders, (conversant) with their own history, knew that force would only (exalt) the (recalcitrant) leader into a martyr and (disenchant) the (compliant) majority.

So the republic chose dialogue over (dominance). It sent not soldiers but a delegation—not (sycophant)s or (pedant)s, but (significant) figures empowered to negotiate a real (covenant). They went to the (verdant) province as (suppliant)s for peace rather than as a (dominant) power issuing a (writ). They would (decant) the grievances honestly—pouring them out in full, leaving the sediment of rhetoric behind—(posit) genuine reforms, and seek a (consonant) resolution rather than a (dissonant) one. The (itinerant) leader, expecting a (mordant) ultimatum, found instead a (complaisant) partner, (conversant) with every grievance he raised.

The negotiations were long and, at times, (petulant). The (recalcitrant) leader, accustomed to (rant), found it harder to be (trenchant) across a table than from a (pulpit). His (penchant) for the (defiant) gesture met the delegation's (vigilant) patience. There were (aberrant) moments—a walkout, a (flippant) insult, a (poignant) plea. A faction of the province's poorest, some reduced to a (mendicant)'s want, pressed their (significant) case; a prosperous faction, whose (luxuriant) lands made them fear the cost of (revolt), urged caution. The republic's negotiators stayed (nonchalant) under provocation, (jubilant) at each small breakthrough.

The resolution was a (significant) (covenant)—a grant of genuine autonomy that left the province self-governing in its own affairs while (consonant) with the republic as a whole. It was no (scant) concession: the capital would no longer be so (dominant), so (mordant), so dismissive. The province would have its (vibrant) voice, its (significant) say. In return, the (recalcitrant) leader agreed to (recant) his call for secession; the threatened (revolt) dissolved. The (covenant) was sealed not by a (tyrant)'s (writ) but by mutual consent, an (affidavit) of good faith on both sides.

Beyond the (poignant) negotiation, ordinary life kept its (fragrant) round. The province's (verdant), (luxuriant) fields yielded an (abundant) harvest; a (fragrant) wind carried the smell of cut hay; a (ruminant) herd grazed the (verdant) slopes. In the capital, a fire crew tested a (hydrant); a janitor applied (disinfectant) to a clinic floor; a chemist mixed a (lubricant) and a (coagulant) in his lab. A (mendicant) sat by the cathedral (pulpit); an (applicant) waited at the courthouse; a (jubilant) crowd raised a (pennant) at a festival. A child wound a (decrepit) old clock; a poet read from a (pulpit); the air after rain was (fragrant) and clean. There was no (occult) menace, no (malignant) (revolt)—only the (resonant), (consonant) peace of a republic that had held.

The episode entered the national (annals) as a lesson in unity without tyranny. A republic, the historians wrote, will always face (recalcitrant) regions, (defiant) voices, (poignant) grievances—and the test is whether it can answer them without becoming (malignant) or (dominant). The (tyrant)'s instinct is to (supplant) and crush; the (pusillanimous) instinct is to surrender. The (valiant) middle path is to negotiate a real (covenant)—to be (vigilant) without being (malignant), (puissant) without being a (tyrant), and to (decant) every grievance honestly, separating substance from sediment, rather than dismiss it as (insignificant).

So Velmoria, one hundred and ninety years free, learns that the unity of a republic is held not by the (dominant) fist but by the (consonant) covenant—that a (recalcitrant) province is best answered not by the (catapult) and the (writ) but by the (vigilant), (complaisant) patience of a people who would rather negotiate than (supplant). The marshal ruled as a (tyrant) who treated every region as a (suppliant) tenant and crushed every (revolt) with (malignant) force. His country, nearly two centuries past his fall, has chosen the (converse)—to hold itself together by consent rather than coercion, to grant the (recalcitrant) a real voice rather than a (mordant) ultimatum, and to keep, beneath all the (poignant), (vibrant) tensions that (beset) any union of free peoples, the one (significant), (resonant) truth that no (tyrant) ever learns: that a republic holds together not because it is (dominant), but because its (covenant) is just—and a just covenant, freely kept, binds a people more firmly than any (writ) a tyrant ever issued, or any false premise he ever (posit)ed.

本批词根亮点：

• -son 家族：resonant（共鸣）/consonant（辅音=共同声音）/dissonant（不和谐）/sonorous（洪亮），全来自拉丁 sonus

• sycophant：原意"告发无花果走私者"，后演变为"拍马屁的人"

• blatant：来自斯宾塞史诗《仙后》中吵闹的怪兽 Blatant Beast，后成形容词"公然嚣张的"

• exorbitant：ex+orbit，"超出轨道的"=过高的，词源形象生动

• recalcitrant：re+calcitrare（用脚后跟踢），一直往后踢=顽固不服从

• abundant：ab+unda（波浪），水浪涌出的=充裕的，inundate（淹没）同根

• recruit：来自 crescere（生长），重新补充生长=招募，crescent（月牙）同根

• jubilant：来自希伯来语"羊角号"（yobel），50年一次吹号庆祝=jubilee（周年庆典）

quiz.question: decrepit ________
quiz.explanation: 破旧的; 衰老的, 破旧的建筑. The decrepit building was scheduled for demolition. 这座破旧的建筑计划拆除。 | de->向下，crep->发出噼啪声（拉丁crepare），decrepit->向下破裂=破旧的（cf. crepuscular黄昏的=光线破碎，decrepitude衰老）

quiz.question: pulpit ______
quiz.explanation: 讲道坛, 讲道坛布道. The minister delivered a pulpit sermon on forgiveness. 牧师在讲道坛上发表了关于宽恕的布道。 | pulpit->讲台（拉丁pulpitum->台），pulpit->布道台（cf. pulp纸浆，pulpit station布道台）

quiz.question: merit ___
quiz.explanation: 功绩; 优点, 绩效工资. Employees receive merit pay based on performance. 员工根据表现获得绩效工资。 | merit->应得（拉丁merere->赚到），merit->功绩/值得（cf. meritorious有功的，meretricious华而不实，demerit缺点）

quiz.question: _____ _ writ
quiz.explanation: 令状; 文书, 发出令状. The court will issue a writ of habeas corpus. 法院将发出人身保护令状。 | writ->写下的东西（古英语writan），writ->法律文书（cf. write写，written书面的，Scripture圣经=写下的）

quiz.question: posit _ ______
quiz.explanation: 假定; 提出, 提出理论. Scientists posit a theory to explain the phenomenon. 科学家提出理论来解释这一现象。 | posit->放置（拉丁ponere），posit->提出假设（cf. position位置，deposit存款，compose作曲，oppose反对）

quiz.question: ____ deposit
quiz.explanation: 存款; 押金, 银行存款. She made a bank deposit of her paycheck. 她将薪水存入了银行存款。 | de->向下，posit->放置，deposit->放到下面=存款/押金（cf. compose作品，impose强加，expose暴露，depose废黜）

quiz.question: ________ circuit
quiz.explanation: 电路; 巡回, 电路. The electric circuit powers all the lights. 电路为所有灯供电。 | circui->绕圈（拉丁circuire），circuit->绕一圈=电路/巡回（cf. circle圆，circulate循环，circumference周长）

quiz.question: _____ conduit
quiz.explanation: 管道; 导管, 水管. The water conduit supplies the entire neighborhood. 水管供应整个社区。 | con->共同，duit->引导（拉丁ducere），conduit->引导流体的管道（cf. conduct传导，duct管道，aqueduct渡槽）

quiz.question: bruit _____
quiz.explanation: 传播; 散布, 四处传播. Do not bruit about confidential information. 不要四处传播机密信息。 | bruit->噪音/传闻（法语bruit->声音），bruit about->到处散布传闻（cf. brute野兽，bruise淤伤，bruit=rumor传播）

quiz.question: recruit ___ _______
quiz.explanation: 招募; 新兵, 招募新成员. The club aims to recruit new members this year. 俱乐部今年旨在招募新成员。 | re->再，cruit->生长（拉丁crescere），recruit->重新补充生长=招募（cf. crescent月牙，accrue积累，increase增加）

quiz.question: _____ affidavit
quiz.explanation: 宣誓书, 宣誓的宣誓书. He signed a sworn affidavit before the notary. 他在公证人面前签署了宣誓书。 | affidavit->他发誓了（拉丁，affidar->信任），affidavit->宣誓陈述书（cf. fidelity忠诚，fiancé未婚夫，confide信任）

quiz.question: _____ wit
quiz.explanation: 机智; 才智, 敏锐的机智. Her sharp wit made her a popular speaker. 她敏锐的机智使她成为受欢迎的演讲者。 | wit->知道（古英语witan），wit->机智/才智（cf. witness目击者=知道的人，witty机智的，witticism妙语）

quiz.question: exalt ______
quiz.explanation: 赞扬; 提升, 赞扬美德. Religious texts often exalt virtue and righteousness. 宗教文本经常赞扬美德和正义。 | ex->向上，alt->高（拉丁altus），exalt->举到高处=赞扬/提升（cf. altitude高度，exaltation兴奋，exalted崇高）

quiz.question: tilt __ _________
quiz.explanation: 倾斜; 攻击, 攻击风车(徒劳的战斗). Do not tilt at windmills like Don Quixote. 不要像堂吉诃德那样攻击风车。 | tilt->倾斜（古英语tealt），tilt at windmills->向风车冲刺=做徒劳的事（堂吉诃德把风车当巨人，cf. tilt倾斜）

quiz.question: _________ bolt
quiz.explanation: 螺栓; 闪电, 闪电. A lightning bolt struck the tree during the storm. 暴风雨期间,闪电击中了树。 | bolt->快速飞出（古英语bolt->箭），bolt->螺栓/闪电/逃跑（cf. bolt from the blue晴天霹雳，deadbolt死锁）

quiz.question: revolt _______
quiz.explanation: 反叛; 起义, 反抗. Citizens began to revolt against the oppressive regime. 公民开始反抗压迫性政权。 | re->回，volt->转（拉丁volvere），revolt->转过身来=反叛（cf. revolve旋转，revolution革命，convoluted复杂）

quiz.question: fault ____
quiz.explanation: 断层; 过错, 断层线. The earthquake occurred along a major fault line. 地震发生在主要断层线上。 | fault->缺陷（拉丁fallere->失败），fault->过错/断层（cf. default默认/违约，default=de+fault，faulty有缺陷的）

quiz.question: ____ vault
quiz.explanation: 金库; 跳跃, 银行金库. Valuables are stored in the bank vault. 贵重物品存放在银行金库中。 | vault->拱形（拉丁volvere->卷），vault->拱形屋顶/金库/跳跃（cf. vault过跳，pole vault撑杆跳，the vault of heaven天穹）

quiz.question: occult ________
quiz.explanation: 神秘的; 玄学的, 神秘实践. Some people study occult practice and mysticism. 有些人研究神秘实践和神秘主义。 | oc->覆盖，cult->隐藏（拉丁celare），occult->被隐藏的=神秘的（cf. conceal隐藏，ceiling天花板，occultation掩星）

quiz.question: ______ catapult
quiz.explanation: 弹射器; 猛然推进, 发射弹射器. Ancient armies would launch catapult projectiles. 古代军队会发射弹射器抛射物。 | kata->向下，pult->抛（希腊pallein），catapult->向下抛射=弹射器（cf. impel推动，propel推进，repel击退）

quiz.question: exult __ _______
quiz.explanation: 欢欣鼓舞; 狂喜, 为胜利欢欣鼓舞. The team will exult in victory after the championship. 球队将在赢得冠军后为胜利欢欣鼓舞。 | ex->向外，ult->跳跃（拉丁salire），exult->向外跳跃=欢欣鼓舞（cf. result结果，insult侮辱，exultation狂喜）

quiz.question: _____ cant
quiz.explanation: 伪善的话; 行话, 虚伪的伪善话. His pious cant fooled no one. 他虚伪的伪善话骗不了任何人。 | cant->歌唱/吟诵（拉丁cantare），cant->宗教式的吟诵=虚伪的说教（cf. chant吟唱，enchant迷住，incantation咒语）

quiz.question: decant ___ ____
quiz.explanation: 倒酒; 倾析, 倒酒. Let the wine breathe, then decant the wine carefully. 让酒透气,然后小心地倒酒。 | de->向下，cant->倾斜/罐（拉丁canthus->罐沿），decant->倾斜倒出=倒酒/倾析（cf. can罐，decanter酒壶）

quiz.question: recant _________
quiz.explanation: 撤回; 放弃, 撤回证词. The witness refused to recant testimony under pressure. 证人拒绝在压力下撤回证词。 | re->回，cant->唱/说，recant->收回所说的话=撤回声明（cf. cant伪善话，chant吟唱，enchant迷住）

quiz.question: _______ mendicant
quiz.explanation: 乞丐; 托钵僧, 乞讨的乞丐. The begging mendicant asked for alms on the street. 乞讨的乞丐在街上乞求施舍。 | mendic->乞讨（拉丁mendicus），mendicant->以乞讨为生的人=乞丐/托钵僧（cf. mendicancy乞讨，beg乞求）

quiz.question: _____________ significant
quiz.explanation: 重要的; 显著的, 统计上显著的. The results were statistically significant at the 0.05 level. 结果在0.05水平上具有统计显著性。 | sign->标记，fic->制造，significant->制造出标记/含义=显著的（cf. sign标志，signal信号，signify意味）

quiz.question: ___ applicant
quiz.explanation: 申请人, 求职者. The job applicant submitted an impressive resume. 求职者提交了一份令人印象深刻的简历。 | apply->申请，cant->做的人（拉丁），applicant->申请的人（cf. apply申请，application申请，applicable适用的）

quiz.question: ______ supplicant
quiz.explanation: 恳求者; 哀求者, 谦卑的恳求者. The humble supplicant begged for mercy. 谦卑的恳求者乞求怜悯。 | sup->向下，plic->折叠/弯曲（拉丁），supplicant->弯下膝盖的人=恳求者（cf. suppliant同义，supplicate跪求，supplant取代）

quiz.question: ______ lubricant
quiz.explanation: 润滑剂, 发动机润滑剂. Regular engine lubricant changes extend vehicle life. 定期更换发动机润滑剂可延长车辆寿命。 | lubric->滑（拉丁lubricus），lubricant->使滑动的物质=润滑剂（cf. lubricate润滑，lubricious好色的=滑腻的）

quiz.question: scant ________
quiz.explanation: 不足的; 缺乏的, 缺乏的证据. There was scant evidence to support the claim. 几乎没有证据支持这一说法。 | scant->短缺（古挪威skamt->短），scant->不足的/吝啬（cf. scanty短缺的，skimp吝啬，scarcely几乎不）

quiz.question: __________ pedant
quiz.explanation: 学究; 书呆子, 吹毛求疵的学究. The nitpicking pedant corrected every minor error. 吹毛求疵的学究纠正每个小错误。 | pedant->教师（意大利pedagogo->教导），pedant->过分拘泥于书本的老师=学究（cf. pedagogy教学法，pedantic学究的）

quiz.question: _______ confidant
quiz.explanation: 密友; 知己, 值得信赖的密友. She shared her secrets with her trusted confidant. 她与她值得信赖的密友分享秘密。 | con->共同，fid->信任（拉丁fides），confidant->可以共同信任的人=知己（cf. confident自信的，confide信赖，fidelity忠诚）

quiz.question: ______ descendant
quiz.explanation: 后裔; 后代, 直系后代. He is a direct descendant of the founder. 他是创始人的直系后代。 | de->向下，scend->爬（拉丁scandere），descendant->向下爬的后代（cf. ascend上升，transcend超越，condescend屈尊）

quiz.question: ________ defendant
quiz.explanation: 被告, 刑事被告. The criminal defendant pleaded not guilty. 刑事被告表示不认罪。 | de->加强，fend->防御（拉丁defendere），defendant->防御自己的人=被告（cf. defend防御，offense进攻，fend off抵挡）

quiz.question: abundant ______
quiz.explanation: 丰富的; 充裕的, 充足的供应. The region has an abundant supply of fresh water. 该地区有充足的淡水供应。 | ab->离开/充满，und->水（拉丁unda->波浪），abundant->水浪涌出的=充裕的（cf. undulate波动，inundate淹没，redundant多余）

quiz.question: verdant _____
quiz.explanation: 翠绿的; 青翠的, 翠绿的田野. Spring rains create verdant field landscapes. 春雨造就了翠绿的田野景观。 | verd->绿（拉丁viridis），verdant->绿意盎然的（cf. verdigris铜绿，verdure青翠，cf. verde西班牙语/意大利语的绿）

quiz.question: mordant ___
quiz.explanation: 尖刻的; 讽刺的, 尖刻的机智. His mordant wit could be quite cutting. 他尖刻的机智可能相当刺耳。 | mord->咬（拉丁mordere），mordant->像咬人一样尖刻的（cf. mordacious尖刻，remorse悔恨=再次咬，morsels碎片=咬一口）

quiz.question: ______ pageant
quiz.explanation: 选美; 盛典, 选美比赛. She competed in the beauty pageant last summer. 她去年夏天参加了选美比赛。 | pageant->活动舞台（中世纪拉丁pagina->舞台），pageant->盛大的展示/选美（cf. page页面，pageantry盛典，display展示）

quiz.question: _____ litigant
quiz.explanation: 诉讼当事人, 民事诉讼当事人. The civil litigant filed a lawsuit for damages. 民事诉讼当事人提起损害赔偿诉讼。 | litig->争论（拉丁lis->诉讼），litigant->参与诉讼的人（cf. litigate诉讼，litigation诉讼，litigious好打官司的）

quiz.question: enchant ___ _____
quiz.explanation: 使着迷; 施魔法, 使人群着迷. The magicians tricks will enchant the crowd. 魔术师的戏法将使人群着迷。 | en->使，chant->歌唱（拉丁cantare），enchant->对着施魔法歌唱=迷住（cf. chant吟唱，disenchant使幻灭，incantation咒语）

quiz.question: penchant ___ ____
quiz.explanation: 嗜好; 倾向, 对爵士乐的嗜好. He has a penchant for jazz music. 他对爵士乐有嗜好。 | penchant->倾斜（法语pencher->倾斜），penchant->心倾向于某物=嗜好（cf. pendant悬挂，pendulum钟摆，depend依赖）

quiz.question: trenchant ________
quiz.explanation: 尖锐的; 鲜明的, 尖锐的批评. The reviewer wrote a trenchant critique of the film. 评论家对这部电影写了尖锐的批评。 | trench->切/挖（法语trancher->切），trenchant->像利刃切割的=尖锐的（cf. trench战壕，entrench巩固，retrench削减）

quiz.question: disenchant ___ ______
quiz.explanation: 使幻想破灭, 使公众幻想破灭. The scandal will disenchant the public with politics. 这起丑闻将使公众对政治幻想破灭。 | dis->去除，enchant->迷住，disenchant->去除魔法=使幻灭（cf. enchant迷住，disenchantment幻灭，disillusioned幻灭的）

quiz.question: __________ sycophant

quiz.question: unrepentant ______
quiz.explanation: 不悔改的; 顽固的, 不悔改的罪人. The unrepentant sinner refused to admit wrongdoing. 不悔改的罪人拒绝承认错误。 | un->否定，repent->悔恨（拉丁poenitere），unrepentant->不悔恨的（cf. penitent悔罪的，repent悔改，penal惩罚）

quiz.question: distant ________
quiz.explanation: 遥远的; 疏远的, 远房亲戚. She met a distant relative at the family reunion. 她在家庭聚会上遇见了一个远房亲戚。 | di->分开，stant->站立（拉丁stare），distant->站得很远的=遥远的（cf. distance距离，instant立即=站在当下，constant恒定）

quiz.question: constant ________
quiz.explanation: 恒定的; 不变的, 恒定速度. The object moved at constant velocity. 物体以恒定速度移动。 | con->一起，stant->站立，constant->始终站在那里=恒定的（cf. instant立即，distant遥远，stance立场，circumstance情况）

quiz.question: _____ extant
quiz.explanation: 现存的; 尚存的, 仍然现存的. The manuscripts are still extant in the library. 这些手稿仍然现存于图书馆中。 | ex->向外，stant->站立，extant->仍然站立着的=现存的（cf. exist存在，extinct灭绝=不再站立，constant恒定）

quiz.question: ________ sextant
quiz.explanation: 六分仪, 航海六分仪. Sailors use a nautical sextant to navigate by stars. 水手使用航海六分仪通过星星导航。 | sext->六分之一（拉丁sextus->第六），sextant->测量六分之一圆弧的仪器=六分仪（cf. sextet六重奏，sixtieth第六十）

quiz.question: piquant ______
quiz.explanation: 辛辣的; 开胃的, 开胃的味道. The sauce has a piquant flavor that tingles the tongue. 这种酱汁有一种刺激舌头的开胃味道。 | piquer->刺（法语），piquant->有刺激性的=辛辣开胃的（cf. pique刺激，pike矛/梭鱼，picket尖桩/警戒线）

quiz.question: ______ truant
quiz.explanation: 逃学者, 逃学者. The school truant was caught by authorities. 逃学者被当局抓获。 | truant->游荡者（威尔士语truan->可怜的人），truant->逃学游荡的人（cf. play truant逃学，vagrant流浪者）

quiz.question: _______ savant
quiz.explanation: 学者; 专家, 博学的学者. The learned savant published groundbreaking research. 博学的学者发表了开创性的研究。 | savant->知道（法语savoir->知道），savant->博学者（cf. savvy精明，sage智者，idiot savant白痴学者）

quiz.question: flamboyant _____
quiz.explanation: 艳丽的; 炫耀的, 炫耀的风格. The artist is known for his flamboyant style. 这位艺术家以其炫耀的风格而闻名。 | flamb->火焰（法语flambe），flamboyant->像火焰一样炫耀的（cf. flame火焰，flambé火焰烹饪，flamingo红鹤=像火焰的鸟）

quiz.question: buoyant ______
quiz.explanation: 有浮力的; 活跃的, 活跃的市场. Investors are optimistic about the buoyant market. 投资者对活跃的市场持乐观态度。 | buoy->浮标（荷兰语boei），buoyant->像浮标一样上浮的=有浮力/活跃的（cf. buoy浮标，buoyancy浮力，float漂浮）

quiz.question: ______ clairvoyant
quiz.explanation: 有透视能力的; 有洞察力的, 有天赋的千里眼. The gifted clairvoyant predicted future events. 有天赋的千里眼预测了未来事件。 | clair->清楚（法语clair->clear），voyant->看（voir->看），clairvoyant->清晰地看=千里眼（cf. clear清楚，voyage旅程，voyeur偷窥者）

quiz.question: cognizant __ ____
quiz.explanation: 认识到的; 察觉到的, 察觉到事实. Everyone should be cognizant of fact before deciding. 每个人在决定前都应该察觉到事实。 | cogniz->知道（拉丁cognoscere），cognizant->已经知晓的=察觉的（cf. cognition认知，recognize认识，incognito匿名）

quiz.question: _______ bent
quiz.explanation: 倾向; 天赋, 天生的倾向. She has a natural bent for mathematics. 她对数学有天生的倾向。 | bent->弯曲的/倾向（bend的过去式），bent->天生的倾向（cf. bend弯曲，unbent坚定，bent on下定决心）

quiz.question: recumbent ____
quiz.explanation: 斜躺的; 卧式的, 卧式自行车. He rides a recumbent bike for exercise. 他骑卧式自行车锻炼。 | re->回，cumb->躺（拉丁cumbere），recumbent->向后躺的=斜躺的（cf. incumbent现任的=肩负，succumb倒下，incubate孵化）

quiz.question: adjacent ____
quiz.explanation: 邻近的; 毗连的, 相邻的房间. The conference is in the adjacent room. 会议在相邻的房间里。 | ad->朝向，jac->躺/靠近（拉丁jacere），adjacent->靠近的=邻近的（cf. adjoin毗连，abject沮丧=被抛在旁边，eject弹出）

quiz.question: maleficent _____
quiz.explanation: 作恶的; 有害的, 邪恶的力量. The maleficent force threatened the kingdom. 邪恶的力量威胁着王国。 | male->坏，fic->做（拉丁），maleficent->做坏事的=作恶的（cf. magnificent宏大，beneficent行善，malefactor罪犯）

quiz.question: magnificent _____
quiz.explanation: 壮丽的; 宏伟的, 壮丽的景色. The mountain offers a magnificent vista of the valley. 山上提供了山谷的壮丽景色。 | magni->大，fic->做，magnificent->做得很大=宏伟的（cf. magnify放大，magnanimous宽宏，maleficent作恶）

quiz.question: reticent _____
quiz.explanation: 沉默寡言的; 含蓄的, 含蓄的微笑. She gave a reticent smile and said nothing. 她露出含蓄的微笑,什么也没说。 | reticent->沉默（拉丁reticere->保持沉默），reticent->少言寡语的（cf. taciturn沉默，reticulate网状的=被网住了嘴，同根）

quiz.question: nascent ________
quiz.explanation: 新生的; 初期的, 新兴产业. The nascent industry shows great potential. 新兴产业显示出巨大潜力。 | nasc->出生（拉丁nasci），nascent->正在出生的=新生的（cf. nature自然，native本土的，nation国家，Renaissance文艺复兴=再生）

quiz.question: renascent ________
quiz.explanation: 复兴的; 新生的, 复兴的产业. The renascent industry recovered from the recession. 复兴的产业从衰退中恢复过来。 | re->再，nasc->出生，renascent->再次出生的=复兴的（cf. nascent新生的，renaissance文艺复兴，natal出生的）

quiz.question: ____ __ descent
quiz.explanation: 下降; 血统, 血统. He traced his line of descent back to royalty. 他追溯自己的血统到皇室。 | de->向下，scend->爬（拉丁scandere），descent->向下爬=下降/血统（cf. ascend上升，transcend超越，descend下降）

quiz.question: iridescent ________
quiz.explanation: 彩虹色的; 色彩斑斓的, 彩虹色的羽毛. The bird displayed iridescent feathers in the sunlight. 鸟在阳光下展示彩虹色的羽毛。 | Iris->彩虹女神（希腊），iridescent->像彩虹女神一样七彩的（cf. iris虹膜，iridium铱元素=彩虹色金属）

quiz.question: quiescent _______
quiz.explanation: 静止的; 不活动的, 静止的火山. The quiescent volcano has not erupted in centuries. 静止的火山几个世纪没有爆发。 | quies->安静（拉丁），quiescent->处于安静状态的=静止的（cf. quiet安静，acquiesce默许，requiem安魂曲）

quiz.question: convalescent ____
quiz.explanation: 康复期的; 恢复健康的, 康复之家. He stayed at a convalescent home after surgery. 手术后他住在康复之家。 | con->加强，val->强壮（拉丁valere），escent->正在变化，convalescent->正在恢复强壮=康复期的（cf. valor勇气，valid有效，prevalent普遍）

quiz.question: adolescent __________
quiz.explanation: 青春期的; 青少年, 青少年心理学. She specializes in adolescent psychology. 她专攻青少年心理学。 | ad->朝向，olesc->成长（拉丁adolescere），adolescent->正在成长的=青少年（cf. adult成人=已经成长，abolish废除=使停止成长）

quiz.question: obsolescent ____
quiz.explanation: 即将过时的; 逐渐废弃的, 即将过时的技术. The obsolescent tech is being phased out. 即将过时的技术正在被淘汰。 | ob->对抗，solesc->成长，obsolescent->反向成长=正在过时的（cf. obsolete过时的，absolute绝对的，dissolve溶解）

quiz.question: evanescent ____
quiz.explanation: 短暂的; 逐渐消失的, 短暂的光辉. The evanescent glow faded at dusk. 短暂的光辉在黄昏时消退。 | e->向外，van->消失（拉丁evanescere），evanescent->消失于无形的=短暂的（cf. vanish消失，vanity虚荣，evaporate蒸发）

quiz.question: fluorescent _____
quiz.explanation: 荧光的, 荧光灯. The office uses fluorescent light for illumination. 办公室使用荧光灯照明。 | fluor->萤石（拉丁fluere->流）,escent->发出，fluorescent->发出荧光的（cf. fluoridate加氟，fluoride氟化物，fluid流体）

quiz.question: __________ antecedent
quiz.explanation: 前情; 先例, 历史先例. The policy has a historical antecedent in earlier laws. 该政策在早期法律中有历史先例。 | ante->之前，ced->走，antecedent->走在前面的=先例/前情（cf. precede先于，cede让步，concede承认，precedent先例）

quiz.question: diffident _____
quiz.explanation: 缺乏自信的; 羞怯的, 羞怯的微笑. She gave a diffident smile when praised. 受到表扬时,她露出羞怯的微笑。 | dif->缺乏，fid->信任（拉丁fides），diffident->对自己缺乏信任=羞怯的（cf. confident自信，fidelity忠诚，fiancé未婚夫）

quiz.question: strident ____
quiz.explanation: 刺耳的; 尖锐的, 尖锐的语气. His strident tone made the argument worse. 他尖锐的语气使争论更糟。 | strid->发出刺耳声（拉丁stridere），strident->发出刺耳声的（cf. stride大步，strife争斗，obstruct阻碍）

quiz.question: resident _____
quiz.explanation: 居民; 常驻的, 外籍居民. As a resident alien, she has certain legal rights. 作为外籍居民,她享有某些法律权利。 | re->回，sid->坐（拉丁sedere），resident->持续坐在那里的=居民（cf. preside主持，reside居住，sediment沉淀物）

quiz.question: _________ dissident
quiz.explanation: 持不同政见者, 政治异见者. The political dissident was imprisoned for his views. 政治异见者因其观点被监禁。 | dis->不同，sid->坐（拉丁sedere），dissident->坐在不同地方的人=异见者（cf. president总统，resident居民，preside主持）

quiz.question: provident ____
quiz.explanation: 有远见的; 节俭的, 公积金. Workers contribute to a provident fund for retirement. 工人为退休缴纳公积金。 | pro->提前，vid->看（拉丁videre），provident->提前看到未来的=有远见的（cf. provide提供，providence天意，improvident无远见）

quiz.question: improvident ________
quiz.explanation: 无远见的; 浪费的, 无远见的支出. His improvident spending left him in debt. 他无远见的支出使他负债累累。 | im->否定，provident->有远见的，improvident->无远见的=浪费的（cf. provident有远见，provide提供，providence天意）

quiz.question: transcendent ______
quiz.explanation: 超越的; 卓越的, 超凡的美丽. The sunset displayed transcendent beauty. 日落展现了超凡的美丽。 | trans->越过，scend->爬，transcendent->超越一切的（cf. descend下降，ascend上升，transcendental超验的）

quiz.question: resplendent _____
quiz.explanation: 华丽的; 灿烂的, 华丽的长袍. The king wore resplendent robes for the ceremony. 国王在典礼上穿着华丽的长袍。 | re->加强，splend->闪耀（拉丁splendere），resplendent->极其闪耀的=华丽的（cf. splendid壮丽，splendor辉煌，refulgent灿烂）

quiz.question: pendent ______
quiz.explanation: 悬挂的; 下垂的, 吊灯. The pendent lights hung from the ceiling. 吊灯悬挂在天花板上。 | pend->悬挂（拉丁pendere），pendent->悬挂着的（cf. pendant吊坠，pendulum钟摆，depend依赖，suspend暂停）

quiz.question: indent _________
quiz.explanation: 缩进; 订货, 缩进段落. Remember to indent paragraph in formal writing. 记得在正式写作中缩进段落。 | in->进入，dent->牙/缺口（拉丁dens），indent->在边缘切出缺口=缩进（cf. dental牙科，dent凹痕，indentation缩进）

quiz.question: ____ despondent
quiz.explanation: 沮丧的; 泄气的, 感到沮丧. She began to feel despondent after repeated failures. 经过反复失败后,她开始感到沮丧。 | de->向下，spond->承诺（拉丁spondere），despondent->承诺崩塌向下=沮丧的（cf. respond回应，correspond通信，sponsor赞助）

quiz.question: ______ respondent
quiz.explanation: 被告; 答复者, 调查对象. Each survey respondent answered 20 questions. 每个调查对象回答了20个问题。 | re->回，spond->承诺/回答，respondent->回答的人=调查对象/被告（cf. respond回应，responsible负责任，correspondent记者）

quiz.question: _______ correspondent
quiz.explanation: 记者; 通讯员, 外国记者. The foreign correspondent reported from the war zone. 外国记者从战区报道。 | cor->共同，respond->回答，correspondent->共同回应的人=通讯员（cf. respond回应，responsible负责，correspond对应）

quiz.question: ardent _________
quiz.explanation: 热情的; 热烈的, 热情的支持者. She is an ardent supporter of environmental causes. 她是环境事业的热情支持者。 | ard->燃烧（拉丁ardere），ardent->燃烧般热情的（cf. arson纵火，ardor热情，arduous艰难=需要燃烧力气的）

quiz.question: impudent ______
quiz.explanation: 无礼的; 放肆的, 无礼的评论. His impudent remark shocked everyone present. 他无礼的评论震惊了在场的所有人。 | im->否定，pud->羞耻（拉丁pudere），impudent->没有羞耻的=无礼的（cf. pudeur羞耻，repudiate否认，pudendum羞处）

quiz.question: prudent ________
quiz.explanation: 谨慎的; 明智的, 谨慎的投资者. The prudent investor diversified his portfolio. 谨慎的投资者分散了他的投资组合。 | prud->有远见（拉丁prudens->看得远），prudent->有远见的=谨慎的（cf. provide提供，jurisprudence法理学=看清法律）

quiz.question: imprudent ________
quiz.explanation: 不谨慎的; 轻率的, 轻率的决定. The imprudent decision cost the company millions. 轻率的决定让公司损失了数百万。 | im->否定，prudent->谨慎，imprudent->不谨慎的（cf. prudent谨慎，jurisprudence法理学，improvident无远见）

quiz.question: ________ reagent
quiz.explanation: 试剂, 化学试剂. The lab ordered a new chemical reagent for testing. 实验室订购了新的化学试剂用于测试。 | re->回，ag->驱动（拉丁agere），reagent->在化学反应中驱动的物质=试剂（cf. react反应，reactant反应物，agent代理人）

quiz.question: _____ regent
quiz.explanation: 摄政者, 皇家摄政. The royal regent ruled until the prince came of age. 皇家摄政统治直到王子成年。 | reg->统治（拉丁regere），regent->代为统治的人=摄政（cf. regal皇家的，regulate调节，regime政权，region地区）

quiz.question: indigent ______
quiz.explanation: 贫困的; 贫穷的, 贫困的家庭. The charity helps indigent family with basic needs. 慈善机构帮助贫困家庭满足基本需求。 | indig->缺乏（拉丁indigere->需要），indigent->非常需要的=贫困的（cf. indigence贫困，indigent vs. indigenous本土的）

quiz.question: intransigent ______
quiz.explanation: 不妥协的; 固执的, 不妥协的立场. His intransigent stance prevented any compromise. 他不妥协的立场阻止了任何妥协。 | in->否定，trans->越过，ig->驱动，intransigent->不肯越步妥协的（cf. transact交易，transit过渡，transaction交易）

quiz.question: exigent _____________
quiz.explanation: 紧急的; 迫切的, 紧急情况. The law allows exceptions for exigent circumstances. 法律允许在紧急情况下例外。 | ex->向外，ig->驱动（拉丁exigere->要求），exigent->强烈要求的=紧迫的（cf. exact精确，examine检查，exiguous稀少）

quiz.question: refulgent _____
quiz.explanation: 灿烂的; 辉煌的, 灿烂的光芒. The diamond sparkled with refulgent light. 钻石闪耀着灿烂的光芒。 | re->加强，fulg->闪光（拉丁fulgere），refulgent->极其闪耀的=灿烂的（cf. fulgent闪耀，effulgent光辉灿烂，cf. fulcrum支点）

quiz.question: effulgent _____
quiz.explanation: 光辉灿烂的, 光辉灿烂的光. The sunrise cast effulgent light across the sky. 日出在天空中投射出光辉灿烂的光。 | ef->向外，fulg->闪光，effulgent->向外射出光芒的=光辉灿烂的（cf. refulgent灿烂，fulgent闪耀，fulgurite闪电石）

quiz.question: plangent ____
quiz.explanation: 响亮的; 悲鸣的, 响亮的音调. The bell rang with a plangent tone. 钟声以响亮的音调响起。 | plang->打击/哭泣（拉丁plangere），plangent->撞击出的响亮悲鸣（cf. complain抱怨=大声哭诉，plaint哀诉，plaintiff原告）

quiz.question: stringent _____
quiz.explanation: 严格的; 严厉的, 严格的规则. The company enforces stringent rules for safety. 公司执行严格的安全规则。 | string->绑紧（拉丁stringere），stringent->绑得很紧的=严格的（cf. astringent收敛，constrict收缩，restrict限制）

quiz.question: astringent _____
quiz.explanation: 收敛的; 涩的, 收敛性化妆水. Use an astringent toner to minimize pores. 使用收敛性化妆水来缩小毛孔。 | ad->朝向，string->绑紧，astringent->朝里绑紧=收敛的（cf. stringent严格，constrict收缩，restrict限制）

quiz.question: contingent _________
quiz.explanation: 可能发生的; 依情况而定的, 或有负债. The company disclosed contingent liability in its report. 公司在报告中披露了或有负债。 | con->共同，ting->触摸（拉丁tangere），contingent->共同接触=有可能接触到的=依情而定的（cf. contagious传染，tangible可触，contact接触）

quiz.question: pungent _____
quiz.explanation: 刺激的; 辛辣的, 刺鼻的气味. The cheese has a pungent smell that is quite strong. 奶酪有一种相当浓烈的刺鼻气味。 | pung->刺（拉丁pungere），pungent->像针刺一样刺激的（cf. puncture刺穿，compunction内疚，poignant辛酸，expunge删除）

quiz.question: cogent ________
quiz.explanation: 有说服力的; 令人信服的, 有说服力的论点. The lawyer presented a cogent argument to the jury. 律师向陪审团提出了有说服力的论点。 | cog->驱动（拉丁cogere->强迫），cogent->强迫人信服的=有说服力的（cf. cogitate思考，coerce强迫，incognito匿名）

quiz.question: divergent _____
